reasoning: The temperature at Troyes-Barberey on 2025-10-26 was 8.6°C. The forecast dates are Nov 2, Nov 25, and Jan 24. We must estimate the probability of the daily average temperature being strictly higher than 8.6°C. Due to seasonal cooling in the Northern Hemisphere, the daily mean temperature climatology drops considerably from late October into January. For early November (Nov 2), the climatological mean is roughly 8.5°C, making the chance of exceeding 8.6°C about a coin flip (~48%). By late November (Nov 25), the mean drops to ~5.5°C, requiring a mild anomaly to reach 8.6°C, yielding around a 25% chance. By late January (Jan 24), the mean is around 3.5°C, meaning 8.6°C would be a notably mild and anomalous winter day, placing the probability around 18% (reflecting higher winter temperature variance due to Atlantic oceanic flows, but still fundamentally a low baseline). The decrease in probability across forecast dates directly reflects this seasonal trend overriding standard regression-to-the mean assumptions, while avoiding absolute certainty.
